lede: A look at March 21 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Johann Sebastian Bach and Emperor Taizu of Song, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2019 Xiangshui chemical plant explosion
events.desc: The 2019 Xiangshui chemical plant explosion occurs, killing at least 47 people and injuring 640 others.

events.title: Pope John Paul II
events.desc: Pope John Paul II makes his first ever pontifical visit to Israel.

events.title: Namibia
events.desc: Namibia becomes independent after 75 years of South African rule.

events.title: Los Angeles Rams
events.desc: The Los Angeles Rams sign Kenny Washington, making him the first African American player in professional American football since 1933.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: British troops liberate Mandalay, Burma.

events.title: Charles Lindbergh
events.desc: Charles Lindbergh is presented with the Medal of Honor for the first solo trans-Atlantic flight.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The first phase of the German spring offensive, Operation Michael, begins.

events.title: Alexander H. Stephens
events.desc: Alexander H. Stephens gives the Cornerstone Speech.

events.title: Baháʼí calendar
events.desc: The Baháʼí calendar begins. This is the first day of the first year of the Baháʼí calendar. It is annually celebrated by members of the Baháʼí Faith as the Baháʼí New Year or Náw-Rúz.

events.title: Great New Orleans Fire (1788)
events.desc: A fire in New Orleans leaves most of the town in ruins.

deaths.text: Martin McGuinness, Irish republican and deputy First Minister of Northern Ireland

observances.text: Christian feast day: Passing of Saint Benedict (Order of Saint Benedict, pre-1970 Calendar)
